mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.09% as of June 2013), the persistent near-zero policy rate keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during the ZIRP period, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 45% of months with FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE of 0.2 percentage points or more signals labor market improvement strong enough to accelerate market expectations of Fed asset-purchase tapering, causing an abrupt repricing of discount rates that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days, consistent with the 2013 taper-tantrum dynamic.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops >=0.2pp, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero at p>0.10 across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS retail sales growth and HOUST housing starts both accelerate simultaneously, the joint signal indicates broad-based consumer and residential demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations for consumer-cyclical and homebuilding-linked sectors rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 20-day forward SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or single-series RSAFS-only signal performs equally well, negating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound regime, if CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, markets interpret this combination as raising the probability of imminent policy normalization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in future rate hikes ahead of actual Fed action.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of trigger instances despite the inflation acceleration signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows within ZIRP-period subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO industrial production reflects expanding real-economy output capacity, which feeds through to corporate earnings growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or negative-growth months outperform positive-growth months on average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong PAYEMS job gains and a falling UNRATE jointly signal robust labor market momentum that markets interpret as accelerating the timeline for Fed tapering, triggering a short-horizon bond-yield spike and equity sell-off over the next 5 trading days, an effect stronger than either indicator alone during 2013's taper-sensitive environment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200k AND UNRATE falls month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following joint triggers are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the joint-signal effect is no stronger than the PAYEMS-only univariate signal, invalidating the interaction assumption.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST housing starts cross above their trailing 12-month high, it signals a durable inflection in residential construction demand that historically leads broader economic recovery and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level exceeds its trailing 12-month maximum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following the breakout trigger underperform the unconditional baseline in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or effect disappears outside the 2012-2013 housing recovery episode.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-stage zero-lower-bound regime with improving labor market data (falling UNRATE, rising PAYEMS) and firming CPIAUCSL inflation, markets increasingly anticipate a shift from monetary easing to tapering, causing the historically positive ZIRP-driven equity tailwind to reverse into negative forward returns as rate-normalization expectations dominate valuation discounting.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND UNRATE declining AND CPIAUCSL YoY rising
regime_hint: late-stage ZIRP transitioning to taper anticipation
failure_condition: Conditional 20-day forward SPX returns under the joint trigger remain positive on average with t-test p<0.05, contradicting the anticipated reversal, or the regime split shows no significant direction flip versus the always-on ZIRP hypothesis (H_001).
